title: Nageshvara Temple
tagline: Nageshvara Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Nageshvara temple complex (also spelt Nagesvara and called Naganatheshvara locally) is located in Begur, a small town within the Bangalore urban district of Karnataka state, In
overview.description: The Nageshvara temple complex (also spelt Nagesvara and called Naganatheshvara locally) is located in Begur, a small town within the Bangalore urban district of Karnataka state, India. Two shrines within the temple complex, the Nageshvara swamy main deity was consecrated by Rishi’s and temple were commissioned during the rule of Western Ganga dynasty kings Nitimarga I (also called Ereganga Neetimarga, r. 843–870) and Ereyappa Nitimarga II (also called Ereganga Neetimarga II, r. 907–921).

topPlaces.name: Inscription stones of Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Inscription stones of Bengaluru refers to numerous historical stone plaques, tablets, and rock carvings bearing inscriptions (epigraphs) and sometimes sculptures (such as hero stones) found throughout the Bengaluru metropolitan area in Karnataka, India. Within the limits of the modern Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike, approximately 175 such stones have been documented in various historical surveys and publications.

topPlaces.name: Begur inscriptions and hero stones
topPlaces.description: The Begur inscriptions and hero stones are significant archaeological and epigraphical artifacts located primarily in Begur, a locality within the Bengaluru South taluk. Spanning a period from the Western Ganga dynasty (c.

topPlaces.name: Begur, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Begur or Begūru is a suburb of Bengaluru in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located off Bengaluru-Hosur highway.

topPlaces.name: Devarachikkanahalli
topPlaces.description: Devarachikkanahalli, abbreviated as DC Halli, is a village in South Bangalore. It is a small locality which is located near Bannerghatta Road, little away from Bilekahalli.

topPlaces.name: Akshay Nagar
topPlaces.description: Akshay Nagar is a neighbourhood in Begur ward in Southeast Bangalore. It is surrounded by Hulimavu to the west and Begur to the east.

topPlaces.name: Kudlu Gate metro station
topPlaces.description: Kudlu Gate is an elevated metro station on the North-South corridor of the Yellow Line of Namma Metro in Bengaluru, India.

topPlaces.name: Singasandra metro station
topPlaces.description: Singasandra is an elevated metro station on the north–south corridor of the Yellow Line of Namma Metro in Bengaluru, India.

topPlaces.name: Vijaya bank layout
topPlaces.description: Vijaya Bank layout or Mulki Sundaram Shetty Nagar is a suburb in Bangalore, India, situated in Bilekahalli off Bannerghatta Road. It is also known as BTM IV stage.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nageshvara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nageshvara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nageshvara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (37 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (13 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nageshvara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nageshvara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nageshvara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Nageshvara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nageshvara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Inscription stones of Bengaluru, Begur inscriptions and hero stones, Begur, Bengaluru, Devarachikkanahalli, Akshay Nagar.
